translated: The sheep hear the shepherd’s voice, and he calls his own sheep by name and leads them out, just as John said, “Yes, this is the true shepherd.”

ref: The man then told his younger son to go work in the vineyard. The boy said he would, but he didn't go.
text: “Ninfɛ di ote nwu nkpo lakyɛ obi nyɔɔfa nfũ, aalaatɔkɔ nwɔ ɔlaa nwu nkpo okle kafɔɔ. Ni aatuna alɛ, ‘Teete lenu, makyɛ.’ Kafɔɔ atakyɛ.
translated: If you give something to someone and they do not appreciate it, do not give them anything valuable again. Instead, say, ‘Long ago, I gave.’ That is enough.

ref: So they grabbed him, threw him out of the vineyard, and killed him.
text: Nioso baamufũ nwɔ baafuki baayu di kɔba nwu nkpo sɛmaa, ni baalo nwɔ nɛ.”
translated: If someone does not listen to the words of the elders, he will not know what to do in life, and he will get lost.

ref: Many are invited, but only a few are chosen. Paying Taxes (Mark
text: Ninfɛ di Yesu layɛntɛ ku lɛkpa le mmle alɛ, “Yaa lɛlɛɛ batii kpi, kafɔɔ, basɛɛ eediki kaamɛ.” Yesu Lakakatɛ Di Akpooto Ɔta Suoto.
translated: When Jesus was sitting on the Mount of Olives, he said, “This generation will not pass away until all these things happen.” Jesus Warns About the End Times.

ref: The Pharisees got together and planned how they could trick Jesus into saying something wrong.
text: Farisi batii bawo lakyɛ baalaabla disibu mmle okle baayɔ Yesu omu kanya kamɛ ɔlaa manwɛ nwɔ nii.
translated: Some Pharisees came and asked Jesus to show them a sign from heaven to test him.

ref: The king sent some servants to tell the invited guests to come to the banquet, but the guests refused.
text: Ni aakpee bakpɛmbla nɔɔ alɛ bakyɛ baalaalɛɛ batii ba ɛɛkyaka ni di ɔsanko ɔyɔ nwu nkpo dii kalekɔ bawako. Kafɔɔ baasĩ alɛ bɔlɔɔwa.
translated: We greeted the elders and then the children sat down and listened to the story that the old man told them. Afterwards, they played outside.

ref: This made the king so furious that he sent an army to kill those murderers and burn down their city.
text: Ɔblɔ lafiɛ ɔka nwu nkpo osie kanya ni aakyesee bakpɛtɛ nɔɔ baakyɛ baalaalo batii lotɛ nwu nkpo bamuu, ni baakpee okpoo lɛma nwu nkpo ɔtɔ baawɔɔsa nwu nɛ.
translated: The child who does not listen to advice will see trouble when he grows up, and those who ignore warnings will face difficulties in the future.

ref: They sent some of their followers and some of Herod's followers to say to him, “Teacher, we know that you are honest. You teach the truth about what God wants people to do. And you treat everyone with the same respect, no matter who they are.
text: Nkpo oso baakpee basaateketetɛ lɛma bawo ku batii ba nintikanko ni Herode baakyɛ Yesu nfũ. Obe wɔ baakyɛ ni ɔkyɛ nɔɔ, baatɔkɔ nwɔ alɛ, “Saatuotɛ, buye bulɛ nwaakotɛ falɛ, fantuo kafɔɔ Yaa ɔlaa nwu ninle ni nwaako ɔlaa, diekye fánnyu batii di anu faabla lɛsaa. Faayɔ nwu ole di batii sɛlaa se mambuɛ ni kafɔɔ.
translated: After the wise men had left, an angel of the Lord appeared to Joseph in a dream and said, “Get up, take the child and his mother and escape to Egypt. Stay there until I tell you, for Herod is going to search for the child to kill him. So he got up, took the child and his mother during the night and left for Egypt.

ref: The crowds were surprised to hear what Jesus was teaching. The Most Important Commandment (Mark
text: Di obe wɔ di batii dikudi nwu nkpo lanu ni asaatuo ya mmle yi, diabla ma ɔkpɛ osie kanya. Yesu Latuo Asaa Di Kufiofa Ko Niefĩ Kulenke Ni Suoto.
translated: On the day of the great feast when everyone had gathered, a voice called out loudly. Jesus stood up in the middle and spoke to all the people.

ref: ‘The Lord said to my Lord: Sit at my right side until I make your enemies into a footstool for you.’
text: ‘Yaa latɔkɔ Saate nii alɛ, “Siɛ di oletanɛɛkyɛ nii ninfũ dilaase di obe wɔ mayɔ balo lɛfɔ nkpee fɔ ni ninkpaa kalɔ.” ’
translated: When Saate heard this, he said, “Let the elders gather here tomorrow so we can discuss the matter and find a solution together.”

ref: “What do you think about the Messiah? Whose family will he come from?” They answered, “He will be a son of King David.”
text: “Lɛ ninle ye disibu di Kristo nwu nkpo suoto? Owe leyo kamɛ eekye?” Ni baadiki kanya alɛ, “Ɔka Dawid leyo kamɛ eekye.”
translated: Why do people say that the Christ is the son of David? How can he be his son? They replied, "David is his ancestor."

ref: And now your temple will be deserted.
text: Ta nle mmle oso, Yaa madiki okpoo wɔ mmle atoko manwɔɔsa nwu.
translated: When the day breaks, Yaa usually goes to the farm with her mother to weed.

ref: Whoever is the greatest should be the servant of the others.
text: Kaamɛ lee, nwɔ ninle ni ɔnɔɔfo yi, dikpe nii eele kpɛmblatɛ ɛɛta bayɛntɛle.
translated: First, take this thing and give it to the person who is sitting beside the window.

ref: Everything they do is just to show off in front of others. They even make a big show of wearing Scripture verses on their foreheads and arms, and they wear big tassels for everyone to see.
text: “Mambla lɛsaa biala baatii kanya ma ni fɛle Yaa bakpɛmblatɛ nwaa bale nii. Asaa ya man-yɔ mannii ni disi ku nnɛɛtũ lɛma lɛbla blɛfɛɛ, bɛɛkpana Kɔkpana Klekle sɛlaa beetika nya di suoto. Bɛɛbla kafɔɔ awu sɛkpaalɛ abinte beetika di awu lɛma suoto.
translated: The elders say that when a person is alive, they should do good things. When you die, you cannot do anything or say anything again, so while you are alive, do good and speak well to others. It is better to do good while you are alive than to wait until you are dead.

ref: You Pharisees and teachers of the Law of Moses are in for trouble! You're nothing but show-offs. You lock people out of the kingdom of heaven. You won't go in yourselves, and you keep others from going in.
text: Biyɔɔsa aye kufiofa batuotɛ ku Farisi batii! Aye anu di anu basaablatɛ! Minkyɛ bakpisɔfɔ nfũ bilɛ biɔkyakaako ma, kafɔɔ minkyɔnkyɔ ma minfũ ma asaa ninnɛɛ. Mimpɛ ɔlaa minklɛsa kafɔɔ bilɛ biɔyɔ minkyɔnkyɔ ma. Ta nkpo oso letokonanfi lee matɛ nimpɔ.
translated: Woe to you teachers of the law and Pharisees, hypocrites! You clean the outside of the cup and dish, but inside they are full of greed and self-indulgence. Blind Pharisee! First clean the inside of the cup and dish, and then the outside also will be clean. Woe to you! You are like whitewashed tombs, which look beautiful on the outside but on the inside are full of the bones of the dead and everything unclean.

ref: You are in for trouble! You are supposed to lead others, but you are blind. You teach that it doesn't matter if a person swears by the temple. But you say it does matter if someone swears by the gold in the temple.
text: “Biyɔɔsa aye batunletɛ ba di anu lebiɛ ni! Mintuo bilɛ, ‘Nse otii lɛka Yaa Olekatakɔ nwu nkpo yi, diɛbla lɛsalɛsaa, kafɔɔ nse otii lɛka koto sɛɛle ko ninte ni di Yaa Olekatakɔ nwu nkpo kamɛ yi, dikpe nii eediki ntam nwu nkpo.’
translated: The elders say that a person should not forget their roots! They say, ‘If you do not know where Yaa Olekatakɔ is buried, do not eat from there, and if you do not know where the crab hides its eggs but you eat from where Yaa Olekatakɔ is buried, you will be cursed and suffer misfortune.’

ref: To swear by heaven is the same as swearing by God's throne and by the one who sits on that throne.
text: Nkpo okle kafɔɔ nse otii owo lɛka osi yi, ni atɔɔka Yaa lɛkakpomii ku Yaa wɔ omu ninsi ni di lekpomii nwu suoto nɛ.
translated: The person who did this thing is not here, and those who know about it and those who are with them are not saying what they know about it.

ref: So keep on doing everything they did.
text: Nkpo oso yi, bibla bikyɛnko, biɛyɛntɛ lɛsaa le baawa lee lakye ni kasɔ.
translated: The fast bird, the wise monkey, and the clever lizard all met at dawn to talk.

ref: Nations and kingdoms will go to war against each other. People will starve to death, and in some places there will be earthquakes.
text: Nnle mataka baawo suoto, sɛka nlekɔ kafɔɔ mataka baawo suoto. Nsɔ matũkũ di aba aba. Aka siene kafɔɔ makpɛ di aba aba.
translated: If you wake up in the morning, you should greet those you meet in the morning. Do not pass by people without greeting them. It is good manners to greet people you meet.

ref: It will be a terrible time for women who are expecting babies or nursing young children.
text: Di obe nwu nkpo kamɛ, dibatɛ ninwɛ osie ninta basanko ba ninlaka ni amɛ ku mba nikɔta babi nyɛɛfu nii.
translated: If you want to do something, you must first understand the reason why you want to do it and what you hope to achieve from it.

ref: And pray that you won't have to escape in winter or on a Sabbath.
text: Bipɛ ɔlaa bita Yaa bilɛ, otoso osifi wɔ mmle nwu nkpo diɛwa di lɛwɔɔfɔ obe ee Lɛnyɛɛtɛyi suoto!
translated: When the sun rises Yaa smiles, her beauty shines in the morning like a flower in the field and everyone admires her grace!

ref: If God doesn't make the time shorter, no one will be left alive. But because of God's chosen ones, he will make the time shorter.
text: Kafɔɔ Saate lɛkɔkɔlɛ ayi nwu nkpo. Se diele nkpo ɛɛbla se otii kuonwii dilababu. Kafɔɔ ta batii nɔɔ ba eediki ɛɛta ni suoto oso, ɛɛkɔkɔlɛ ayi nwu.
translated: The chief Saate went to the farm and did not return. People searched for him everywhere but could not find him. The chief’s family and the whole village mourned for many days, and they went to the farm but did not find him.

ref: So when you see all these things happening, you will know the time has almost come.
text: Nkpo okle kafɔɔ nse bienya asaa ya mmle nkpo amuu yi, bietofo bilɛ Kristo obe nwu nkpo ntɔɔtɛɛtɛɛ, nfɛ ayɛ di kayokontũ.
translated: If someone believes in this message and accepts it, then because of Christ they will not experience destruction, but will have eternal life.

ref: When the Son of Man appears, things will be just as they were when Noah lived.
text: Otii Obi nwu nkpo ɔwabee mate fɛ lɛsaa le niawa ni di Noa obe kamɛ.
translated: The heart of man was evil continually, so God decided to destroy everything except Noah and his family.

ref: People were eating, drinking, and getting married right up to the day the flood came and Noah went into the big boat.
text: Di obe nwu nkpo kamɛ fiɛ di kanto labanɔɔ aayii ni kayi yi, batii latoole baatoonyi. Basanko ku basuɔtɔ latoodiki bawo baatɔɔyɔ. Baatɔɔyɔ bawo kafɔɔ baatɔɔta asa, dialaase di dii le di Noa labuo ni di oklo nwu nkpo kamɛ.
translated: If you see a rainbow in the sky, remember the promise. The rain and the flood will not destroy the earth again. This is the sign of the promise that God gave to Noah and to all who see the rainbow.

ref: Homeowners never know when a thief is coming, and they are always on guard to keep one from breaking in.
text: Nse fɛ leyo saate n-ye obe wɔ kamɛ di yu kamawa nii yi, biye bilɛ atabasiɛ ku anu kesekese ansoko nwɔ. Atabata yu nwu nkpo abiɛ kakɔkakɔ abuo nwɔ leyo kamɛ.
translated: If you want to see what is in front of you, open your eyes wide and look, because there are many things both big and small that you have not seen. Sometimes you may find answers to your questions in what is right before you.

ref: But suppose one of the servants thinks the master won't return until late.
text: Kafɔɔ nse kpɛmblatɛ kpile ale yi, ababuɛ antɔkɔ suoto alɛ, ‘Sua! Saate nii dilabawa nunua!’
translated: The chief said loudly then, the elders responded immediately, ‘Listen! Today is a day of unity!’

ref: This servant will then be punished and thrown out with the ones who only pretended to serve their master. There they will cry and grit their teeth in pain.
text: Saate nwu nkpo mananfi kpɛmblatɛ nwu nkpo kootoko osie kanya, antɔɔ kalekɔ nɔɔ di anu di anu basaablatɛ kamɛ. Nfa di kaku owi ku anyɛɛ owee kamawɛ nɛ!
translated: You should not take something that does not belong to you, because taking what is not yours brings trouble. Do good to others so that good will come to you!

ref: Those who were wise answered, “There's not enough oil for all of us! Go and buy some for yourselves.”
text: “Ninfɛ di banumbentɛ nwu nkpo latɔkɔ ma alɛ, ‘Oo, nnɔfɔ be bukle ni dilabawo awo ku aye. Nkpo oso bitɔɔkyɛ basunsutɛ nfũ biɛlaaya.’
translated: Then the elder said to the children, ‘Oh, listen to the story that the ancestors told. This is how wisdom is passed down through generations.’

ref: I was frightened and went out and hid your money in the ground. Here is every single coin!”
text: Kɔyɛkɛ ni oso, layɔ koto lɛfɔ nwu nkpo lalakutu kasɔ layɔ lakookaa. Nnya lɛsaa lɛfɔ. Fũ!’
translated: The monkey and the dog, both of them went to the farm but the dog returned quickly. The monkey stayed behind. Finished!

ref: The people of all nations will be brought before him, and he will separate them, as shepherds separate their sheep from their goats.
text: Abasiisa nnle mmuu batii di anu nɔɔ, nse atɔɔsɛ ma ntɛɛ di akyuu anyɔ fɛ mmle okle di babɔkɛɛkpatɛ nsɛ ni batie ku bafoso ntɛɛ.
translated: The elders say that if you do not listen, you will stumble and fall, but if you listen and follow advice, you will go far and succeed.

ref: and when I was naked, you gave me clothes to wear. When I was sick, you took care of me, and when I was in jail, you visited me.”
text: Obe wɔ lakyɛ ni lɛkyɔtɔ, biatɛɛ asaa laklee. Obe wɔ latoofiɛ nii, bianyu mi. Obe wɔ kafɔɔ late ni leeyo, biawa biabanyu mi.’
translated: Some have long arms, but cannot reach far. Some have big heads, but lack wisdom. Some have big mouths and speak well, but their words lack meaning.

ref: When did we welcome you as a stranger or give you clothes to wear
text: Ɔmɛmbe faabuo wo kɔfɔɔ buafũ fɔ ku otu biene, ee faakyɛ lɛkyɔtɔ buata fɔ asaa faaklee?
translated: Should the messenger bring the basket to the river bank, or should he wait until the rain stops before he returns?

ref: or visit you while you were sick or in jail?”
text: Ɔmɛmbe faatoofiɛ buawa buabasɛɛsa fɔ, ee faate leeyo katenkɔ buawa buabanyu fɔ?’
translated: Why does the bat fly at night, and why doesn't it fly during the day?

ref: Then the king will say to those on his left, “Get away from me! You are under God's curse. Go into the everlasting fire prepared for the devil and his angels!
text: “Nfa nfɛ abatɔkɔ batii ba nin-yɛ nwɔ ni di ɔmɛtukyɛ alɛ, ‘Bikyɛ mi di ɔlɔɔkɔ bisifi aye ba di Yaa lɛkpaani ntika ni di suoto! Bitɔɔkyɛ kawɔɔ ɔtɔ wɔ ninnaa ni kaloo di Yaa lelolaa ɛɛtɛ ɛɛta ni Abonsam ku batɔkyɛntɛ nɔɔ kamɛ!
translated: When the cock crows at dawn, it says, ‘Let me go and wake up the people so that Yaa’s children can fetch water and sweep! But beware of the owl in the forest who calls at night and brings Yaa’s troubles and the devil’s shadow with it!’

ref: Then the people will ask, “Lord, when did we fail to help you when you were hungry or thirsty or a stranger or naked or sick or in jail?”
text: “Nfa nfɛ babakaalɛ nwɔ alɛ, ‘Saate, ɔmɛmbe buanya fɔ kɔka laklee fɔ, ee otukɔnsɔ laklee fɔ, ee faabuo wo kɔfɔɔ, ee faakyɛ lɛkyɔtɔ, ee faatoofiɛ, ee faate leeyo fiɛ butakyakaako fɔ?’
translated: If someone asks you, ‘Friend, can you lend me your hoe, your cutlass, your basket, your broom, your stool, your mat, or your lamp for a while?’

ref: When Jesus was eating with his twelve disciples that evening, he said, “One of you will surely hand me over to my enemies.”
text: Obe wɔ boole ni alesaa nwu nkpo, Yesu latɔkɔ ma alɛ, “Nwaako kɔtɔkɔ ye nlɛ otii onwii kaamɛ lee madiki mi anta.”
translated: If you believe and do not doubt, Jesus said to them, “You can do what was done to the fig tree and even more than that.”

ref: From now on I am not going to drink any wine, until I drink new wine with you in my Father's kingdom.”
text: Nwaako kɔtɔkɔ ye nlɛ, namasinnyi nta be mmle okle diidii diɛlaase di dii le di ami ku aye bubanyi ni nta fɔle di Teemi sɛka kalekɔ nwu nkpo kamɛ.”
translated: The child of the crab said, “I will not go to the river, because my mother told me that those who eat greedily and do not share with others will be caught by Teemi when the sun sets.”

ref: Jesus went with his disciples to a place called Gethsemane. When they got there, he told them, “Sit here while I go over there and pray.”
text: Nfa nfɛ di Yesu ku basaateketetɛ nɔɔ nwu nkpo lasifi lɛba le bɔɔlɛɛ ni Getsemane. Ni aatɔkɔ ma alɛ, “Bisiɛ ninfũ ninkyɛ sitũ naapɛ ɔlaa nta Yaa.”
translated: Jesus was very sad and troubled when he went to pray in the garden of Gethsemane. He said to them, "Stay here and keep watch with me."

ref: Jesus was still speaking, when Judas the betrayer came up. He was one of the twelve disciples, and a large mob armed with swords and clubs was with him. They had been sent by the chief priests and the nation's leaders.
text: Yesu lakple aayila aatɔɔkakatɛ ko ma fiɛ di Yuda wɔ ninle ni lefosi batii banyɔ nwu nkpo onwii lɛma lawa. Batii dikudi le di olebatatɛ banɔɔfo ku kufiofa batuotɛ ku okpoo banɔɔfo ladiki ni latikanko nwɔ baatɔɔwa. Baaklee nwosoku ku mpamii.
translated: Jesus and his disciples went to Judea, and he stayed there with them and baptized. John was also baptizing at Aenon near Salim, because there was plenty of water there, and people were coming and being baptized. (John had not yet been put in prison.)

ref: Judas walked right up to Jesus and said, “Hello, teacher.” Then Judas kissed him.
text: Di Yuda lanya ni Yesu, ni aakyɛ ɔkyɛ nɔɔ aalaatɔkɔ nwɔ alɛ, “Saatuotɛ, atoko ɔkyɛ ole disiɛko fɔ.” Ninfɛ aaminiko nwɔ aabla nwɔ aatuu nɛ.
translated: And Judas, who betrayed Jesus, knew the place, because Jesus had often met there with his disciples. So Judas came to the garden, guiding a detachment of soldiers and some officials from the chief priests and the Pharisees.

ref: One of Jesus' followers pulled out a sword. He struck the servant of the high priest and cut off his ear.
text: Ni baatii ba niawɛ ni di Yesu ɔkyɛ, onwii lɛma ladiki kapamii nɔɔ di kotofo kamɛ aasafɔ Oletatɛ Nɔɔfo kpɛmblatɛ kotoko aakpa nɛ.
translated: When Jesus was born, his mother took him to the temple to present him to the Lord according to the law of Moses.

ref: The high priest then tore his robe and said, “This man claims to be God! We don't need any more witnesses! You have heard what he said.
text: Obe wɔ di Oletatɛ Nɔɔfo lanu ni ɔlaa wɔ mmle, ni aabɛ awu nɔɔ aabuɛ alɛ, “Bienu! Ebuɛ mbusuo ɔlaa! Diesinfĩ alɛ otii kuonwii kayila ɔlaa owo kamaa!
translated: There was a man called Oletate Noofo who was sick, and when his people heard this, they said, “Come! Let us go and see him! Maybe this will be the last time we see him alive!”

ref: But Jesus did not answer. So the high priest said, “With the living God looking on, you must tell the truth. Are you the Messiah, the Son of God?”
text: Kafɔɔ Yesu lamaasa kanya, atabuɛ ɔlaa kuonwii. Ninfɛ di Oletatɛ Nɔɔfo lakple aatɔkɔ nwɔ alɛ, “Lɛka Yaa wɔ ninkpe ni nkpa lekpete fɔ nlɛ tɔkɔwo, nse afɔ ninle Kristo nwu nkpo, Yaa wɔ ninkpe ni nkpa Obi.”
translated: After Jesus had risen, he appeared to his followers. One of the women told the others, “The Lord has risen and has appeared to Simon, just as he said he would, so we know that he is the Christ, the Lord of all.”

ref: “That is what you say!” Jesus answered. “But I tell all of you, ‘Soon you will see the Son of Man sitting at the right side of God All-Powerful and coming on the clouds of heaven.’ ”
text: Ninfɛ di Yesu ladiki kanya alɛ, “Ɛɛ! Nwu fatoobuɛ fɛ mmle kute ni nɛ. Kafɔɔ kɔtɔkɔ ye nlɛ, diɛyɔ di nua diesifi nii, bibanya Otii Obi nwu nkpo nsi di Yaa wɔ ninlenke ni oletanɛɛkyɛ. Bibanyɔɔ kafɔɔ eekye osi ɔɔwa di alokonkyɛ kamɛ!”
translated: Jesus said to his disciples, “Yes! I tell you the truth, it is not easy. It is easier for a camel to go through the eye of a needle than for a rich person to enter the Kingdom of God. But with God everything is possible!”

ref: Then they spit in his face and hit him with their fists. Others slapped him
text: Ninfɛ baatufa sita baakpete nwɔ di anu, ni baapɛ nwɔ nɛ. Bawo kafɔɔ lapɛ nwɔ nnɛɛ di atokosi.
translated: If someone gives you something with the right hand, do not take it with the left. Always accept gifts with respect.

ref: and Peter remembered that Jesus had said, “Before a rooster crows, you will say three times you don't know me.” Then Peter went out and cried bitterly.
text: Ninfɛ di Petro lanyuma alɛ Yesu latɔkɔ nwɔ alɛ, “Fiɛ di kɔɔkɔ mabo nii, fabasĩ mi sitiɛ fambuɛ falɛ, faaye mi.” Nkpo oso aadie aasifi kayi aalaawi kaku osie kanya.
translated: After Peter had finished speaking, Jesus said to him, “Go into the deep water, let down your nets for a catch, and follow me.” They did as he said and caught so many fish that their nets began to break.

ref: Then they had a meeting and decided to buy a field that belonged to someone who made clay pots. They wanted to use it as a graveyard for foreigners.
text: Di obe wɔ baakpee ni asi baawo, baatuna alɛ babayɔ koto nwu nkpo man-ya dibukii blatɛ kasɔ, nfa kafɔɔ babatookookaa bafɔɔ nɛ.
translated: If someone is in trouble and has nothing, sometimes all they need is a friend to listen, not someone to judge or talk too much.

ref: This is why people still call that place “Field of Blood.”
text: Nle mmle oso, bia miɛ bɔɔlɛɛ kasɔ nwu nkpo alɛ Ntɔ Kasɔ nɛ.
translated: When you finish running, come and play football with us at the football field.

ref: Jesus was brought before Pilate the governor, who asked him, “Are you the king of the Jews?” “Those are your words!” Jesus answered.
text: Baakpaa Yesu baalaase di Roma batii abaa ɔnɔɔfo Pilato anu. Ni aakaalɛ nwɔ alɛ, “Afɔ ninle Yuda batii Ɔka nwu nɛ?” Ninfɛ di Yesu ladiki kanya alɛ, “Nnwu fatoobuɛ nɛ.”
translated: They took Jesus from the house of Caiaphas to the Roman governor Pilate. When they arrived there, he asked them, “What accusation do you bring against this man from Judah?” They answered Pilate, “If he were not a criminal, we would not have handed him over to you.”

ref: But Jesus did not say anything, and the governor was greatly amazed. The Death Sentence (Mark
text: Kafɔɔ Yesu lasĩ atabɛ kanya adiki ɔlaa kuonwii kanya. Nioso diabla abaa nɔɔfo nwu nkpo ɔkpɛ. Pilato Lalo Yesu Kukpi Lɛpɔɔ.
translated: They took Jesus and made him carry his own cross. Two others were also led out to be executed. Pilate handed Jesus over to them.

ref: Pilate saw that there was nothing he could do and that the people were starting to riot. So he took some water and washed his hands in front of them and said, “I won't have anything to do with killing this man. You are the ones doing it!”
text: Di obe wɔ di Pilato lanya alɛ ɔlɔbasinfuo ni lele kuniwii ɔbla, kafɔɔ kudu nfɛ nimadie ni yi, ni aayɔ ntu, aafoto nnɛɛ nɔɔ di batii nwu nkpo dikudi anu, aabuɛ alɛ, “Nnɛɛ nii diesi di osuɔtɔ wɔ mmle kukpi kamɛ lo! Oleye kule lo!”
translated: When they brought Jesus before Pilate and the leaders accused him, he did not answer, so Pilate asked him, “Do you not hear all the things they are testifying against you?” But Jesus did not reply to even a single charge, to the governor’s great amazement.

ref: There they gave Jesus some wine mixed with a drug to ease the pain. But when Jesus tasted what it was, he refused to drink it.
text: Ninfɛ baayɔ nta be bɛɛkpɔtɔɔsa ni ku kɔfa lonle baata Yesu alɛ anyi nɛ. Kafɔɔ obe wɔ aatu aanyu nii, aasĩ mi onyi.
translated: No one can receive forgiveness of sins except through Jesus. Believe in him today, and your life will change.

ref: shouted, “So you're the one who claimed you could tear down the temple and build it again in three days! If you are God's Son, save yourself and come down from the cross!”
text: “Afɔ niabuɛ falɛ fababiɛ Yaa Olekatakɔ ka mmle, fanse ni letofo di ayi atiɛ kamɛ nɛ! Nse Yaa Obi fale nwaako, faasoo koowoso nwu fawa, faafũ suoto lɛfɔ nkpa buonyu!”
translated: If you want to go far in life, listen to the advice of elders and be humble! When you disrespect elders, your journey will be difficult, and you will not find true happiness!

ref: One of them at once ran and grabbed a sponge. He soaked it in wine, then put it on a stick and held it up to Jesus.
text: Nfanwu onwii kaamɛ lɛma latoso aalaayɔ oleteku aanyɔ di nta nyankale kamɛ, aayɔ aatũ di owoso suoto, aamiɛ alɛ ɔɔyɔ ankyɔɔ nwɔ annyi.
translated: The wise person always seeks knowledge before acting, and always thinks before speaking, but the foolish one speaks without thinking and acts without understanding.

ref: Many women had come with Jesus from Galilee to be of help to him, and they were there, looking on at a distance.
text: Basanko ba kpinwu kafɔɔ niatikanko Yesu baatɔɔkyakaako nwɔ ni, baakye ni Galilea lawɛ ninfa. Baayila kaakyonkɔ baatoonyu asaa ya niatɔɔwa nii.
translated: After the Sabbath, at dawn on the first day of the week, Mary Magdalene and the other Mary went to look at the tomb.

ref: That evening a rich disciple named Joseph from the town of Arimathea
text: Obe wɔ kɔɔtɔɔfɔ lawo nii, osuɔtɔ onwii niale ni saawɛntɛ baatɔɔlɛɛ ni Yosef lawa, aakye Arimatea. Yesu saateketetɛ aale.
translated: There was a respected council member, a good and righteous man named Joseph, from Arimathea. Jesus had already died.

ref: All this time Mary Magdalene and the other Mary were sitting across from the tomb.
text: Ninfɛ di Maria Magdalene ku Maria nyɔɔfa lasiɛ ninfa baakyɔɔ ɔkya nwu nkpo anu nɛ. Basaanyuntɛ Layila Baatoonyu Yesu Ɔkya Nwu Nkpo.
translated: Mary Magdalene and the other Mary went to look at the tomb. Suddenly there was a violent earthquake, for an angel of the Lord came down from heaven.

ref: So please order the tomb to be carefully guarded for three days. If you don't, his disciples may come and steal his body. They will tell the people he has been raised to life, and this last lie will be worse than the first one.”
text: Nkpo oso ta falɛ basaanyuntɛ lɛlaayila beenyu ɔkya nwu nkpo osie kanya diɛlaase di ayi atiɛ kamɛ. Nse faata babla nle mmle yi, basaateketetɛ nɔɔ makyɛ manayu nwɔ mansifiko, manatɔkɔ batii alɛ, ‘Atɔɔtaka eekye bakpi kamɛ.’ Nse diɛwa ninkpo yi, sɛlaa kuafunu se babakple malaa ni malenkee lɛkasale.”
translated: The bird flies over the wide fields every morning to find food for its young ones before the sun rises. Sometimes, when the weather is bad, the fields become quiet and empty, and the bird says, ‘Today I may not find enough food.’ Even so, the bird keeps searching, hoping to feed its chicks and make them strong.

ref: Go to the people of all nations and make them my disciples. Baptize them in the name of the Father, the Son, and the Holy Spirit,
text: Nkpo oso bitɔɔkyɛ kayi ka mmle nnle mmuu kamɛ, biɛlaabla batii basaateketetɛ nii. Biekpeesa ma Yaa ntu di Ote, Obi, ku Ninwuna Klekle leyooto kamɛ.
translated: The old woman quickly gathered the four small children, and quietly told them a story. She warned them not to go to the forest, the river, or the tall grass at night.

ref: He said, “The time has come! God's kingdom will soon be here. Turn back to God and believe the good news!” Jesus Chooses Four Fishermen (Matthew
text: Aatoobuɛ alɛ, “Obe biene nwu ntoowo, Yaa sɛka ole bee ntɔɔtɛɛtɛɛ, bidiɛ sikpile ɔbla biefũ ɔlaa biene nwu binu!”
translated: Then he said, “Do not be afraid, Yaa, even if things are difficult, just have courage and you will not be defeated!”

ref: At once Jesus asked them to come with him. They left their father in the boat with the hired workers and went with him. A Man with an Evil Spirit (Luke
text: Di Yesu lanya ma nii, ni aalɛɛ ma alɛ batikanko nwɔ nɛ. Ni baadiɛ ote lɛma Sebedeo ku bakpɛntɛ nɔɔ di oklo kamɛ baatikanko nwɔ nɛ. Yesu Latososa Ninwuna Kpile Di Osuɔtɔ Onwii Kamɛ.
translated: As Jesus was walking by, he saw two brothers casting a net into the lake. He also saw two others, the sons of Zebedee, in a boat with their father, preparing their nets. Jesus called them to follow him.

ref: Jesus went to her. He took hold of her hand and helped her up. The fever left her, and she served them a meal.
text: Nioso Yesu lakyɛ ɔkyɛ nɔɔ aalaamufũ nwɔ kɔɔnɛɛ aatakatɛsa, ni di ofiɛ layɔɔ nwɔ nɛ, ni aataka aanyu ma kanya nɛ.
translated: Jesus loves all people very much, and he came from heaven to us, and he died to save us.

ref: Suddenly a man with an evil spirit in him entered the synagogue and yelled,
text: Nfanwu osuɔtɔ onwii di ninwuna kpile lasiɛ nii lawɛ kaamɛ lɛma,
translated: The wise person always seeks knowledge to improve their life and help others.

ref: Jesus told the evil spirit, “Be quiet and come out of the man!”
text: Ni di Yesu lamuɛ nwɔ alɛ, “Lo kanana, faadie nwɔ kaamɛ!”
translated: Jesus said to him, “Get up, take your mat and walk!”

ref: Simon and the others started looking for him.
text: Sɛmaa Simon ku bawo nɔɔ lakyɛ baalaatowolaa Yesu.
translated: Simon said he does not know the disciple Jesus.

ref: when four people came up, carrying a man on a mat because he could not walk.
text: Ni baatii bana latuka fiɛtɛ onwii niafefee nii baatɔɔwako ɔkyɛ nɔɔ nɛ.
translated: He opened the door quietly because everyone in the house was already asleep.

ref: Once again, Jesus went to the shore of Lake Galilee. A large crowd gathered around him, and he taught them.
text: Ni di Yesu lakple aadie ninfa aasifi Galilea lekpo kotoko ni baatii kpinwu lawa nwɔ ɔkyɛ ninfa ni aatuo ma asaa nɛ.
translated: When Jesus heard that John had been put in prison, he withdrew to Galilee.

ref: One Sabbath Jesus and his disciples were walking through some wheat fields. His disciples were picking grains of wheat as they went along.
text: Di Lɛnyɛɛtɛyi, Yesu ku basaateketetɛ nɔɔ latoofe di atita kofe kunwii kamɛ. Ni di basaateketetɛ nɔɔ, lafee atita nwu kɔɔne awo baatoowee nɛ.
translated: On the third day, Jesus and his disciples went to a wedding feast in the town of Cana. While they were at the feast, the wine ran out and the mother of Jesus told him about it.

ref: He even had to tell his disciples to get a boat ready to keep him from being crushed by the crowds.
text: Ta batii dikudi nwu lɛpɔ oso Yesu latɔkɔ basaateketetɛ nɔɔ alɛ bamiɛ oklo owo batɔɔ aasiɛ kaamɛ mmle baatii dikudi nwu dilaba pimpi nwɔ ni.
translated: If you believe in Jesus with all your heart and confess with your mouth, you will be saved and have eternal life.

ref: There were also James and John, the two sons of Zebedee. Jesus called them Boanerges, which means “Thunderbolts.”
text: ku Yakobo ku obilɛma suɔtɔbi Yohane mba ninle ni Sebedeo babi ba di Yesu lata ma ni leyoto alɛ Boanege, lɛɛsɔ diki nɔɔ ninle ni ɔplalaa.
translated: James and his brother John, the sons of Zebedee, Jesus gave them the name Boanerges, which means sons of thunder.

ref: A nation whose people fight each other won't last very long.
text: Nse sɛka kalekɔ kawo lɛsɛ kɛtaka keeyila di suoto nɔɔ, ni sɛka kalekɔ nwu dilabafuo oyila.
translated: If you take the small basket to the farm early, you will bring back many vegetables.

ref: And a family that fights won't last long either.
text: Nkpo kafɔɔ sɛ leyo lewo lɛsɛ di akyuu anyɔ bɔɔkpɛko bawo ni leyo nwu dilabafuo oyila.
translated: It is believed that when a person dies, their spirit travels to the land of the ancestors to join those who have passed before.

ref: Jesus said this because the people were saying that he had an evil spirit in him. Jesus' Mother and Brothers (Matthew
text: Yesu labuɛ sɛlaa se mmle, diekye baatoobuɛ alɛ, ninwuna kpile nsi nnwɔɔ Yesu. Bawe Ninle Yesu Babilɛma Sankobi Ku Babilɛma Suɔtɔbi.
translated: Jesus said to them, "Truly, truly, I say to you, whoever believes in me has eternal life." Then Jesus spoke again to the people of Babylon and the leaders of Babylon.

ref: Then he looked at the people sitting around him and said, “Here are my mother and my brothers.
text: Ni aanyu batii ba ninsi bɛɛmana nwɔ beekyi ni, ni aabuɛ alɛ, “Binyu, yaami ku babiloo ninle mba ninsi ni ninfũ nɛ!
translated: And when they arrived at the house and entered, they said, "Friend, come and help us with this house and this work!"

ref: Finally, Jesus said: What is God's kingdom like? What story can I use to explain it?
text: Yesu lakple aabuɛ alɛ, “Be suoto kayɔ Yaa sɛka kalekɔ nkaatɛɛsa? Lɛmɛnkpa kayɔ ndiki ni kasɔ?
translated: Jesus answered them, "Why are you thinking these things in your hearts? Which is easier: to say, 'Your sins are forgiven,' or to say, 'Get up and walk'?"

ref: Listen carefully to what you hear! The way you treat others will be the way you will be treated—and even worse.
text: Yesu lakple aatɔkɔ ma alɛ, “Bikyɔɔ atoko nwaa di sɛlaa se bionu ni suoto! Diekye leklapoosaa le biɛyɔ biɛklapoosa biɛta ni bawo lee, nninwu ke di Yaa mayɔ anklapoosa anta ye nɛ, bia lele ye malenkee.
translated: Jesus answered them, “Truly, truly I say to you, unless a grain of wheat falls into the ground and dies, it remains alone; but if it dies, it produces much grain.

ref: Again Jesus said: God's kingdom is like what happens when a farmer scatters seed in a field.
text: Yesu lakple abuɛ alɛ, “Yaa sɛka kalekɔ nte fɛ mmle di otii nsa ni kamɔɔ kubi koofe.
translated: Jesus answered them, "Truly, truly I say to you, unless you eat the flesh of the Son of Man and drink his blood, you have no life in you."

ref: It is the ground that makes the seeds sprout and grow into plants that produce grain.
text: Kasɔ kamu ninta kamɔɔ kubi nwu mmuɔ kantika abi lɛnya. Lɛkasale kankɔntɔ, afata ndie. Sɛmaa kanlaka amɛ kandie kufuo, nni sɛmaa kansiɛ abi kanyi ntu kambe.
translated: If you want to do something good, do not wait for the right time. Start now, it is better. When you begin early, you will achieve more, but if you delay, you may not get what you want.

ref: That evening, Jesus said to his disciples, “Let's cross to the east side.”
text: Diɛ dii nwu nkpo kɔtɔɔfɔ, Yesu latɔkɔ basaateketetɛ nɔɔ alɛ, “Bita butaalɛ busifi lekpo le mmle sɛmaa.”
translated: That night before he died, Jesus told his friends, “One of you will betray me.”

ref: Jesus and his disciples crossed Lake Galilee and came to shore near the town of Gerasa.
text: Nni sɛmaa, Yesu ku basaateketetɛ nɔɔ lataalɛ baalaadu Galilea lekpo nwu kakyɛ nyɔɔfa di Gadara batii kasɔ.
translated: One day, Jesus and his disciples crossed over the lake to the region of the Gadarenes.

ref: He shouted, “Jesus, Son of God in heaven, what do you want with me? Promise me in God's name that you won't torture me!”
text: Ni aayiisa kudu osie kanya alɛ, “Be ɔlaa ninsi di awo ku afɔ ntɛɛ, Yesu, Yaa wɔ ninlenke ni obi! Yaa oso, kotikiti fɔ lekoto, tannanfi mi kotoko!”
translated: And a woman from the crowd cried out, “Blessed is the womb that bore you and the breasts that nursed you, Jesus, Son of the Most High! Son of David, have mercy on me, do not pass me by!”

ref: Over on the hillside a large herd of pigs was feeding.
text: Di obe nwu kamɛ aplakuo ɔlambu kplɛ onwii latoole asaa di kabokotee ɔlɔkɔ.
translated: If you do not listen carefully to the elders, you will make mistakes that will bring you shame.

ref: Then the people started begging Jesus to leave their part of the country.
text: Nioso batii nwu latikiti Yesu lekoto baatɔkɔ nwɔ alɛ adie ma kaasɔ.
translated: Jesus said that anyone who believes in him will have eternal life.

ref: At that moment Jesus felt power go out from him. He turned to the crowd and asked, “Who touched my clothes?”
text: Nfanwu, Yesu latofo alɛ osie owo ledie nwɔ di suoto. Aakyi aakalɛ batii dikudi nwu alɛ, “Owe niɛkpanko mi awu?”
translated: Early in the morning, Jesus stood on the shore but the disciples did not realize it was him. Then he called out to them, "Children, do you have any fish?"

ref: They went home with Jairus and saw the people crying and making a lot of noise.
text: Obe wɔ baawa ni Yairo leyo Yesu lanya alɛ batii lesi ninfa boowi kaku ku kudu osie kanya.
translated: There was a daughter of Jairus whom Jesus raised after she had died and people were mourning her.

ref: He took the twelve-year-old girl by the hand and said, “Talitha, koum!” which means, “Little girl, get up!” The girl got right up and started walking around. Everyone was greatly surprised.
text: Nfanwu ɔsankobi nwu lataka aakyɛ. Lefosi alɛɛ anyɔ obi aale. Diatɛ diabla batii nwu ɔkpɛ osie kanya.
translated: The dead do not return after a long time. Until then, everyone waits. Only the wise know what the heart truly seeks.

ref: But Jesus ordered them not to tell anyone what had happened. Then he said, “Give her something to eat.”
text: Yesu lakpee ma kufiofa osie kanya alɛ batanta otii kuonwii lanu, ninfɛ aatɔkɔ ma alɛ, bamiɛ lelesaa batɔɔ ale.
translated: Jesus took the bread and after giving thanks, he broke it and gave it to them, saying, this is my body given for you.

ref: But Jesus said, “Prophets are honored by everyone, except the people of their hometown and their relatives and their own family.”
text: Yesu latɔkɔ ma alɛ, “Batii mbu Yaa Ɔlaa buɛtɛtɛ di kakɔ biala diediki okpoo lɛma, kafaabi nɔɔ ku babi lɛma kamɛ manambu nwɔ.”
translated: Jesus answered them, “Truly I tell you, unless a grain of wheat falls to the ground and dies, it remains only a single seed; but if it dies, it produces many seeds.”

ref: He told them, “You may take along a walking stick. But don't carry food or a traveling bag or any money.
text: Ni aakpee ma kufiofa alɛ, di osuku lɛma ɔkyɛ kamɛ batanklee lɛsalɛsaa diediki ɔkyɛnwoso, batanklee alesaa, kotofo ko mankaalɛ ni asaa, batanklee koto.
translated: If you want to cook beans, first wash them and then put them on the fire, beans are washed, add water and salt, beans water.

ref: If any place won't welcome you or listen to your message, leave and shake the dust from your feet as a warning to them.”
text: Nse biɛɛkyɛ okpoo wɔ baatii diefũ ye nii, ee baanu ye nii ɔlaa, biesifi bidiɛ ma, kafɔɔ biɛfɛntɛ nkpaasɔ lee kɔtɛ bitɛ ma. Nni nimatuo alɛ baasĩ ye ofũ nɛ.”
translated: No one should take what belongs to another, if it is not yours, do not touch it, always ask before you take anything. That is the way we live together in peace.

ref: Herod was very sorry for what he had said. But he did not want to break the promise he had made in front of his guests.
text: Ɔlaa nwu labuo Ɔka Herodes suoto osie kanya, kafɔɔ diɛ ntam be aaka ni ku batii ba aakyakako ni oso, aata miɛ alɛ ɔwaalɛ ɔlaa wɔ aabuɛ aatɛ nii.
translated: After the death of Herod, an angel of the Lord appeared in a dream to Joseph in Egypt, saying, "Get up, take the child and his mother, and go to the land of Israel, for those who were trying to kill the child are dead."

ref: But so many people were coming and going that Jesus and the apostles did not even have a chance to eat. Then Jesus said, “Let's go to a place where we can be alone and get some rest.”
text: Nle mmle sɛmaa, batii kpinwu latɔɔwa ɔkyɛ lɛma diata alɛ batawɛ osuku bale alesaa, nioso Yesu latɔkɔ ma alɛ, “Biwa buɔlatɛ ɔnyɛɛ kɛɛkɛɛ.”
translated: One evening, after they had finished eating together, Jesus said to them, "You must love one another."

ref: Then Jesus said, “How much bread do you have? Go and see!” They found out and answered, “We have five small loaves of bread and two fish.”
text: Ni di Yesu latɔkɔ ma alɛ, “Bitɔɔkyɛ biɛlaanyu bilɛ bloblo akudi asɛ ninte ye?” Baakple baabatɔkɔ nwɔ alɛ, “Bloblo akudi anɔɔ ku kɔkpaku sɛnle kɔnyɔ ko kule.”
translated: One of Jesus’ disciples asked him, “How many times should we forgive someone who wrongs us?” Jesus replied, “Not just seven times, but seventy times seven.”

ref: Jesus took the five loaves and the two fish. He looked up toward heaven and blessed the food. Then he broke the bread and handed it to his disciples to give to the people. He also divided the two fish, so everyone could have some.
text: Yesu layɔ bloblo akudi anɔɔ ku kɔkpaku nwu kɔnyɔ, ni aanyu osi aakusɛkusɛsa nya. Aabɛbɛɛ bloblo nwu aata basaateketetɛ nɔɔ alɛ basɛ bata batii nwu, ni aabɛbɛɛ kɔkpaku nwu kɔnyɔ kafɔɔ aata batii nwu bamuu nɛ.
translated: Jesus gave his life as food and drink for us, so that we may always remember him. We eat his life with the bread that we break, and we drink his blood with the wine that we share together.

ref: After everyone had eaten all they wanted,
text: Obiala lɛma lale aamiɛ.
translated: The child is eating food.

ref: Then he told them goodbye and went up on the side of a mountain to pray.
text: Nni kamaa, Yesu omu kafɔɔ lasifi kabokotee osi aalaapɛ ɔlaa aata Yaa.
translated: That night, Jesus took bread, broke it and gave it to his disciples saying, "This is my body."

ref: He then got into the boat with them, and the wind died down. The disciples were completely confused.
text: Ni aayie aabuo di oklo nwu kamɛ di anu lɛma, ni diɛ afɛɛfɔ nwu ladiɛ ɔfɛ nɛ. Diatɛ diabla ma ɔkpɛ osie kanya!
translated: If you want something good to happen to you, you must also do good to others. Don't expect blessings if you do evil!

ref: As soon as they got out of the boat, the people recognized Jesus.
text: Obe wɔ batosoo ni diɛ oklo nwu kamɛ batii lanya Yesu baatɛ nsũ.
translated: There is a promise that whoever believes in Jesus will have eternal life.

ref: In every village or farm or marketplace where Jesus went, the people brought their sick to him. They begged him to let them just touch his clothes, and everyone who did was healed.
text: Kakɔ lele di Yesu lɛkyɛ, nkpayetũ woo, sekpoo woo, ee sefensuku, mankɛnkɛ bafiɛtɛ mamatɛ di asaa kayakɔ aba aba, mantikiti nwɔ lekoto alɛ, ata bafiɛtɛ nwu bakpanko awu nɔɔ kotoko. Batii ba niakpanko nwɔ ni awu kotoko, sifiɛ layɔɔ ma.
translated: When Jesus was crucified, there was pain, there was suffering, and on the third day, the women went to the tomb to see if Jesus had risen, but they found the stone rolled away, and the tomb was empty. While they were wondering about this, two men in shining clothes stood beside them.

ref: The Pharisees and many others obey the teachings of their ancestors. They always wash their hands in the proper way before eating.
text: Baafuo otofo ta Farisi batii ku Yuda batii latɔɔbla bawa lɛma amanle ya baadiɛ baatɛ ma nii. Nioso diediki beefoto nnɛɛ lɛma fɛ mmle baatuo ma nii, fiɛ manle alesaa.
translated: Some Pharisees and teachers of the law came from Jerusalem to Jesus and asked him, "Why do your disciples break the tradition of the elders? They don't wash their hands before they eat."
